ref: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
text: Agi Kusɔliɔ baka ʋidi ba ni Farisianɛ ma benu Yesu yɛ abee liɛ ɛ, betè si ba su azeegu kunugu yo,
translated: When Jesus entered Capernaum, a centurion came to him, asking for help.

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
text: Idisami kunukpadzeo kwikɔ lɛ kani ʋa pɔ̀ kúvu kumu ipɔnɔ ɔ.
translated: The chief's palace is the biggest house in the village.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Mlatè ba gani ba kiboebitɛɛ mɛ. Ɔdɔ̃ nɔ liho le liʋíyo wain yɛ ɛ, nite ke fife e oʋí yo esebi ŋaŋa a.
translated: The elders are sitting under the baobab tree. They are discussing the issue of the child's marriage, but the child's mother does not agree.

ref: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
text: Lɛ Yesu agù yɛ abee si lɛ loso e, mleze ni aŋu lɛ ese linu si mléte liwe le e loo ɔnyɔsasa nɔ ɔ, gi maadziba ɛ. Yesu ebu abee ni banɔsumu tiata ba su
translated: When Jesus saw that they were doing this, he took a rope and made a whip, then drove all of them, including the sheep and cattle, out of the temple. He also poured out the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.

ref: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
text: Lɛ Yesu agù yɛ liboe le si, “Lizemɔniwe le ɛ, bɛbitɛ lɛ ɛki banɔ kupɛ̀pɛ, bábitɛ banima aki Lizemɔniwe le e.
translated: When Jesus saw him, he said, "Behold, a true Israelite in whom there is no deceit."

text: Lɛ gi dzeni ye adɔ xe inipe le iyi i igbigba a, xe liwɔ gagla lɛ lifu i ihali ligba lɛ ɛ, liki si ligba ɛlɛ klɔ ʋa likpa gbloo.” Yesu abasi bidɔ ni kigagla a
translated: When the people heard this, they were amazed at his teaching. But Jesus immediately said to them, "Do not be afraid."

ref: The men said to Jesus, “Don't you hear what those children are saying?” “Yes, I do!” Jesus answered. “Don't you know that the Scriptures say, ‘Children and infants will sing praises’?”
text: Lɛ loso beʋi Yesu si, “Dani woenu eboa la gi banuvɔ ba ya bɛɛpani i?” Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Ee menu.” “Mlaʋíkla Aya liboe le oʋídi si, ‘Mlɛ basɛ banuvɔ ba nì bebi ba sì biaki kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ liɔ gi kwiflɔ na a?’ ”
translated: When the disciples of Jesus asked, "Do you want us to prepare the Passover meal for you?" Jesus replied to them, "Yes." He said, "Go into the city to a certain man and tell him, 'The Teacher says: My time is near. I will celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.'"

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Pɔ̀ gi Yosef enu si Arxelao yɛ ɛɛŋa kukusi yo ní yɛka Herodes kutse o kede ní Yudea ɛ, ese lifu ne ki niklɔ kutrɛtrɛ. Agi bɛkpɛ yɛ ɔsã ní kulilaa mɛ loso e, ekyi atrɛ ní Galilea kesea mɛ.
translated: When Joseph learned that Archelaus was ruling over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. After being warned in a dream, he went to the region of Galilee.

ref: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
text: Apani asi banima sì, “Ɔniliɛ gi aaba mɛ kede e, ekeme kura sani mɛ. Lɛ mágbɛ gi maaze yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ ní kapɛa tɔtɔ mɛ ɛ.
translated: I told my mother, "If I had known you were coming, I would have waited for you. I was just going to the farm to pick some okra."

text: Lɛ akɔ lɛ atrɔ ní kitɛɛ aʋa a xe akɔ amanɔ ɛ. Akɔ linɔtukpo le aki ɔdzɛʋi yɛ, lɛ yɛ fɛ akɔ lɛ aki yene e.
translated: There was a man who had two wives. The man loved one wife more, and always gave her things.

text: Lɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ onete ebu le onu ki ba mɛ tole si, ‘Ta kutu, ɔʋiaa. Mába wɔ kura. Wɔtutɔ, wɔdimɛ si, wɔawa liwe tile axwɛ ɛ ki liwe le liʋɛ nɛ.
translated: If a person does not respect you and you go to their house, they will say, "Come, child. I was waiting for you. Sit down, let's talk, we will eat food today and food tomorrow too."

translated: But you have a custom that I should release one man for you at the Passover. So do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?” They cried out again, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber. Then Pilate took Jesus and flogged him.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: “Lɛ loso e, mleze ní aŋu plɛplɛ mɛ, lɛ ese linu si mléte liwe lɛ klɔ gi mlɔ Ɔkaɛ aaba a.
translated: When the chief died, his children did not want to bury him, they said they would put him in a room and lock the door.
